candidate_summary: Per-head RoPE frequency band pre-allocation + L2-norm Q/K constraint + token-tessitura routing
rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7
content_words: ci suuf ci kow ardin tunings

independent_scoring.title: Frequency Bands in RoPE (ICLR 2026)
independent_scoring.sem_justification: Directly studies per-head preferential RoPE band usage; same domain & object (RoPE frequency bands) and same observed effect (per-head specialisation). Strong semantic overlap with components (1).
independent_scoring.func_justification: Twin observation: per-head band preference in RoPE. Lacks L2-norm constraint and tessitura routing, but the central PER-HEAD BAND mechanism is observed.

independent_scoring.title: Mixed-Frequency RoPE LLMs
independent_scoring.sem_justification: Head-specific RoPE frequency sets via greedy elite-set selection — direct match to per-head pre-allocation component.
independent_scoring.func_justification: EXACT TWIN of component (1). Assigns disjoint/elite frequency sets per head. The remaining XALAM-FREQ deltas (L2 norm + tessitura) are secondary additions on this same mechanism.

independent_scoring.title: Q-ROAR Rethinking RoPE Scaling Quantized LLM
independent_scoring.sem_justification: Partitions RoPE channels into contiguous log-frequency bands with per-band rescaling — overlap with band-partition core.
independent_scoring.func_justification: Functional twin of partitioning-into-bands mechanism; differs in motivation (quantization) but the band-partition device is the same.

independent_scoring.title: DroPE Extending Context by Dropping Positional Embeddings
independent_scoring.sem_justification: Same family (RoPE manipulation for LLMs) but different mechanism (dropping vs band-allocation).
independent_scoring.func_justification: Different functional effect (context extension via dropping). Below functional threshold.

independent_scoring.title: Hallucination Detection Spectral Features (EMNLP 2025)
independent_scoring.sem_justification: Spectral feature framing in LLMs; weak but at threshold.
independent_scoring.func_justification: Spectral features for detection, not attention allocation. Below functional threshold.

independent_scoring.title: Spectral Scaling Laws Language Models (EMNLP 2025)
independent_scoring.sem_justification: Spectral analysis of LLM widths; intersects band-partition perspective.
independent_scoring.func_justification: Spectral rank utilization can be cast as band-allocation perspective; just above threshold.

independent_scoring.title: Local Spectral Attention Speech Enhancement (2023)
independent_scoring.sem_justification: Per-band attention design paradigm; speech domain but same mechanism shape.
independent_scoring.func_justification: Per-band attention design overlapping with band-allocation paradigm. Candidate explicitly lists this as prior art it tries to differentiate from.

independent_scoring.title: Multi-Token Attention (2025/04)
independent_scoring.sem_justification: Head-specialisation variants in attention; partial overlap with per-head pre-allocation.
independent_scoring.func_justification: Head specialisation patterns cover per-head allocation device at a generic level.

agreement_with_07_hit_miss.notes: Independent scoring confirms 8/8 hits. Minor cosine deviations (<=0.02) and functional deviations (<=0.02) do not change hit status. Mixed-Frequency RoPE remains the strongest exact twin.

verdict_reason: total_hits=8 (>=1). Per-head RoPE band pre-allocation and band-partition mechanisms are well-covered prior art (Mixed-Frequency RoPE = exact twin; Frequency Bands in RoPE ICLR 2026 and Q-ROAR are direct twins). Candidate is a recombination of established components — not novel.
